author: yingjialong
description: Allows users to upload local files to the cloud, reducing the burden on local vaults and enabling seamless synchronization across multiple devices.

manifest.author: majiajun
manifest.description: Managing attachments across devices. Automatically uploads your attachments to the cloud and smartly updates all your markdown links, keeping your notes and attachments perfectly organized. Whether you're handling images, PDFs, or any other files, it works seamlessly in the background. You can even use it as a powerful image hosting solution, supporting plugin-provided cloud storage or custom S3 storage!
manifest.name: Cloud Storage

name: Cloud Storage

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's New

  ### Account Recovery Tool

  We've added a built-in recovery tool that helps you reconnect with your OBCS cloud data. If you ever switch devices, reinstall Obsidian, or simply need to set up your account again, OBCS can now automatically scan your
   vault for existing cloud links and restore your account configuration — no manual steps required.

  - Smart Vault Scanning: Automatically detects existing OBCS cloud links in your notes
  - Guided Recovery Flow: A simple step-by-step process to restore account access
  - Batch Support: Handles multiple accounts in a single session
  - Recovery Report: Generates a detailed summary after the process completes

  ### Improved Login Experience

  The login flow now provides clearer guidance when setting up on a new device. If your account needs to be reconfigured, the plugin will walk you through the appropriate steps automatically.

  ### Other Improvements

  - Enhanced build pipeline for more reliable releases
  - Improved timeout handling for long-running operations
  - Minor UI text improvements throughout the settings panel
  - General code quality and stability improvements

- If you feel that images load very slowly in Obsidian, you can try to turn on this option, but it may not be effective.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes 1.5.31
- Added a new "Monitor Subfolders" toggle in the plugin settings
- When enabled, the plugin will monitor attachments in all subfolders of your selected folders
- When disabled (default), only attachments in the directly selected folders will be monitored

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes 1.3.25
## 🔧 Improvements
- Optimize concurrent upload logic



# Release Notes 1.3.24
## 🚀 New Features
**Automatic Attachment Upload**
- Implemented automated file upload functionality
- Enhanced user experience with seamless attachment handling
- Supports various file types and formats

**Configurable Upload Settings**
- Added toggle switch for enabling/disabling automatic uploads
- Introduced granular control over upload behavior
- Accessible through Settings panel

**Upload Size Management**
- Implemented configurable file size threshold settings
- Added size validation before automatic upload
- User-definable maximum file size limits

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Obsidian Cloud Attachments v1.2.21

## 🎉 Highlights

- **Custom S3 Storage Support**: Users can now configure and use their own S3-compatible storage services for attachment uploads
 - Although it doesn't affect uploading, you need to set the bucket to public access in order to successfully preview images in Obsidian.
- Support uploading attachments on a single page

## ✨ New Features

- Added support for custom S3-compatible storage services
- Implemented configuration UI for S3 storage settings:
  - Endpoint URL
  - Region
  - Bucket name
  - Access key and Secret key
- Added validation for S3 connection settings
- Introduced automatic bucket existence check during configuration

## 🔧 Improvements

- Enhanced error handling for upload failures
- Improved upload progress indication
- Optimized connection management for S3 services
- Added detailed logging for troubleshooting

releases.description:
# Obsidian Cloud Attachments v1.2.17

## 🎉 Highlights

- **Custom S3 Storage Support**: Users can now configure and use their own S3-compatible storage services for attachment uploads
- Although it doesn't affect uploading, you need to set the bucket to public access in order to successfully preview images in Obsidian.

## ✨ New Features

- Added support for custom S3-compatible storage services
- Implemented configuration UI for S3 storage settings:
  - Endpoint URL
  - Region
  - Bucket name
  - Access key and Secret key
- Added validation for S3 connection settings
- Introduced automatic bucket existence check during configuration
- Added support for path prefix in S3 bucket

## 🔧 Improvements

- Enhanced error handling for upload failures
- Improved upload progress indication
- Optimized connection management for S3 services
- Added detailed logging for troubleshooting

releases.description: **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/yingjialong/obsidian-CloudStorage/compare/0.1.8...1.1.8

releases.author: yingjialong
releases.description:
We're excited to announce the first release of Obsidian Cloud Storage! This plugin seamlessly integrates cloud storage capabilities into your Obsidian workflow, allowing you to easily upload attachments to the cloud and access them across devices.

Key Features:
- Automatic cloud upload of attachments from specified folders
- Smart link updating in your notes
- Flexible storage options (plugin-provided or custom S3-compatible)
- File filtering and size limit controls
- Comprehensive storage management

This initial release lays the foundation for a more connected and efficient note-taking experience. We appreciate any feedback and look forward to continually improving the plugin.

Thank you for your support and happy note-taking!
releases.name: v0.1.8 - Initial Release of Obsidian Cloud Storage
